chapter_title.en: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types

parts.text: سَأَلَ زُرَارَةُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ :
اَلْمُحْرِمِ يَقَعُ اَلذُّبَابُ عَلَى وَجْهِهِ حِينَ يُرِيدُ اَلنَّوْمَ فَيَمْنَعُهُ مِنَ اَلنَّوْمِ أَ يُغَطِّي وَجْهَهُ إِذَا أَرَادَ أَنْ يَنَامَ

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2687 - Zurara asked Abu Ja'far (as) about a person in the state of ihram who has flies landing on their face, preventing them from sleeping, and whether they may cover their face when they wish to sleep.
Imam (as) said: "Yes."
